steps.title: Set up the rectangular-prism surface area
steps.content: Find the missing base width from base area divided by base length. Then use the rectangular-prism surface-area formula $SA=2(lw+lh+wh)$.

steps.title: Find the width of the base
steps.content:
Base area = length × width, so:
$$30t = \frac{5}{2} \times w \implies w = 30t \times \frac{2}{5} = 12t \text{ cm}$$
Dimensions: l = 5/2 cm, w = 12t cm, h = 12 cm.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$60t+120$’: This treats all four lateral faces as length-height faces: $2(30t)+4(30)=60t+120$, so it does not account for the width-height faces. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$348t+60$’: Equals 2(174t + 30) = 2(30t + 30 + 144t), accounting for all three pairs of faces. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘$1,860t+60$’: Far too large; likely from multiplying by the length instead of dividing by it when finding the width. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$360t$’: Has no constant term, but lh = 30 is a constant, so the surface area must include a constant. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
